highlights.text: On Wednesday at 4pm I wrote: “The bull case tomorrow is simply that ES can resume up to backtest 6766 again.
My lean was they could stating Thursday 4pm: “In the most severe bull case ES will hold above 6766 and flag out above there/6772.
This has been a squeeze for the ages - up 215 points now since last Wednesday’s Failed Breakdown long/low - and we were on board for it.
My core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
However, there was a little interesting context that gave this squeeze an extra push, and that is the Failed Breakdown propelled ES to break out of a two week bullish triangle pattern.

As stated, this squeeze was routine in the sense it had the same cause that produces every single squeeze in ES: Price goes elevator down, sells rapidly, undercuts a low, institutions trap shorts beneath the low, take a massive position, then price squeezes.
I follow institutions in long after the low recovers and my core setup (the Failed Breakdown) prints on the chart.
I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “The best setup here is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s 6694 daily low.
Ideally wait for 6702 to recover before hopping in here.” We saw exactly this at 2pm Wednesday We flushed 6648 down to 6690, Failed Breakdown, ripped into the close last Wednesday to 6740.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown we had last Wednesday that got us long for this ~200 point leg up (we’ve got a very volatile week coming, and we will see plenty just like this).
Then we likely head to all time highs after .” This played out perfectly and we closed up Thursday at 6782.

If it clears we can progress to the next batch of upside targets were are 6875-77, then 6900.” We got right to work on this last evening spending last evening mostly at 6875 and pushing to 6900+ by the close.

post_title: The Parabolic Breakout Rally Continues in SPX, But Will Earnings and FOMC De-Rail It? Oct 28 Plan.
